results.description: Reweighing adjusts instance influence across protected groups before model fitting to reduce structural imbalance in outcomes. Fairness improved under DPD; accuracy trade-off could not be estimated from available signals.

results.description: Disparate Impact Remover repairs group-conditioned outcome distortions to reduce indirect bias patterns. Fairness improved under DPD; accuracy trade-off could not be estimated from available signals.

Finds the decision threshold per group minimising |rate−36.3%| (global median) + 0.5×(1−accuracy) on model-predicted probabilities. Fairness improved under DPD; accuracy trade-off could not be estimated from available signals.

Reject Option Classification shifts uncertain boundary decisions to favor disadvantaged groups while preserving utility. Fairness improved under DPD; accuracy trade-off could not be estimated from available signals.

mitigation.trade_off_summary: Bias changed from 71.8 to 60.9 (↓10.9 pts, 15.2% magnitude). DPD changed 0.3281 → 0.1312; DIR tracked from 0.4513 to 0.7386.  Estimated accuracy after mitigation: 52.3%. Fairness improved under selected metrics. No method guarantees perfect fairness.

audit_result.summary:
The system exhibits a critical bias score of 71.8 with high reliability, indicating significant fairness concerns in selection decisions.

Key disparities include a 32.8% higher selection rate for 'Metro' (59.8%) compared to 'Rural' (27.0%), and a Disparate Impact Ratio of 0.451.

The Theil index of 0.0550 confirms substantial outcome inequality across 'Metro', 'Rural', and 'Semi-Urban' regions.
key_findings: The overall bias score is 71.8 (Critical), indicating significant fairness issues.
'Metro' region has a 59.80% selection rate, 32.8% higher than 'Rural' (26.99%).
The Disparate Impact Ratio is 0.4513, falling below the 0.80 legal threshold.
The Theil inequality index of 0.0550 confirms significant outcome inequality.
'Rural' region's selection rate (26.99%) is less than half of 'Metro' (59.80%).
recommendations: Investigate the underlying causes for the 32.8% selection rate difference between 'Metro' and 'Rural'.
Review selection criteria to ensure they do not inadvertently disadvantage 'Rural' and 'Semi-Urban' regions.
Implement fairness-aware interventions to increase the Disparate Impact Ratio above 0.80.
Monitor the Theil index to reduce outcome inequality across all 'Region' groups.

plain_language.overall: The system exhibits critical bias with a score of 71.8, indicating significant unfairness in selection decisions. Specifically, the 'Metro' region is selected at a much higher rate than 'Rural' and 'Semi-Urban' regions. This disparity is substantial and requires immediate attention.
plain_language.demographic_parity_difference: There is a 32.81% difference in selection rates between the most and least favored regions.
plain_language.disparate_impact_ratio: The selection rate for the 'Rural' region is only 45.13% of the 'Metro' region's rate.
plain_language.statistical_test: The observed disparities in selection rates between regions are statistically significant, indicating they are unlikely due to chance.
plain_language.theil_index: The Theil Index of 0.0550 quantifies the inequality in selection outcomes across different regions.
plain_language.performance_gap: The Performance Gap of 0.68 indicates a significant disparity in overall outcomes across groups.
